l0_summary: Use on-farm storage to control local basis and capture processor premiums, protecting your farm from algorithmic futures volatility and tight global merchant margins.
l1_summary:
- Leverage on-farm storage: Use unencumbered storage to avoid forced harvest sales, dictating when to move grain to capture logistical premiums and basis appreciation.
- Monitor local processor bids: Capitalize on localized basis spikes driven by processors needing immediate supply to run plants, rather than relying on algorithm-distorted futures markets.
- Target traceable ingredient premiums: Shift from bulk commodities to certified, traceable "ingredients" to capture margin premiums and meet evolving buyer demands.
- Navigate global price anchors: Recognize that cheap global origins cap North American export prices; aggressive local basis management is crucial.
- Prioritize margin over speculation: With futures decoupled from physical realities, focus on disciplined risk management, yield improvements, and basis signals over flat price rallies.
